assessment_items.title: Acknowledge Receipt
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD promptly and automatically acknowledge the receipt of messages received from FedRAMP in their FedRAMP Security Inbox.

assessment_items.title: Complete Required Actions
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST complete the required actions in Emergency or Emergency Test designated messages sent by FedRAMP within the timeframe included in the message.

assessment_items.title: Emergency Message Routing
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST route Emergency designated messages sent by FedRAMP to a senior security official for their awareness.

assessment_items.title: Important Message Actions
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD complete the required actions in Important designated messages sent by FedRAMP within the timeframe specified in the message.

assessment_items.title: Maintain a FedRAMP Security Inbox
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST establish and maintain an email address to receive messages from FedRAMP; this inbox is a FedRAMP Security Inbox (FSI).

assessment_items.title: Notification of Changes
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST immediately notify FedRAMP of any changes to the email address for their FedRAMP Security Inbox.

assessment_items.title: Receive Email Without Disruption
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST receive and react to email messages from FedRAMP without disruption and without requiring additional actions from FedRAMP.

assessment_items.title: Trust @fedramp.gov and @gsa.gov
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST treat any email originating from an @fedramp.gov or @gsa.gov email address as if it was sent from FedRAMP by default; if such a message is confirmed to originate from someone other than FedRAMP then the FedRAMP Security Inbox rules no longer apply.

assessment_items.title: Anonymized Feedback Summary
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply an anonymized and desensitized summary of the feedback, questions, and answers about each Ongoing Certification Report as an addendum to the Ongoing Certification Report OR in the next Ongoing Certification Report.

assessment_items.title: Report Availability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply an Ongoing Certification Report to all necessary parties every 3 months, covering the entire period since the previous summary, in a consistent format that is human readable; this report MUST include high-level summaries of at least the following information:

assessment_items.title: Feedback Mechanism
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply an asynchronous mechanism for all necessary parties to provide feedback or ask questions about each Ongoing Certification Report.

assessment_items.title: Limit Sensitive Information
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST NOT irresponsibly disclose sensitive information in an Ongoing Certification Report that would likely have an adverse effect on the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Next Report Date
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply the target date for their next Ongoing Certification Report with other public FedRAMP Certification Data.

assessment_items.title: Responsible Public Certification Report Sharing
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY responsibly supply some or all of the information an Ongoing Certification Report to the public or other parties if the provider determines doing so will NOT likely have an adverse effect on the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Spread Out Reports
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD establish a regular 3 month cycle for Ongoing Certification Reports that is spread out from the beginning, middle, or end of each quarter.

assessment_items.title: Additional Content
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD supply additional information in Quarterly Reviews that the provider determines is of interest, use, or otherwise relevant to agencies.

assessment_items.title: Quarterly Review Meeting
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications MUST host a synchronous Quarterly Review every 3 months, open to all necessary parties, to review aspects of the most recent Ongoing Certification Reports that the provider determines are of the most relevance to agencies.

assessment_items.title: No Irresponsible Disclosure
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST NOT irresponsibly disclose sensitive information in a Quarterly Review that would likely have an adverse effect on the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Next Review Date
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST publicly supply the target date for their next Quarterly Review with other public FedRAMP Certification Data.

assessment_items.title: Meeting Registration Info
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply either a registration link or a downloadable calendar file with meeting information for Quarterly Reviews to all necessary parties.

assessment_items.title: Restrict Third Parties
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD NOT invite third parties to attend Quarterly Reviews intended for agencies unless they have specific relevance.

assessment_items.title: Record/Transcribe Reviews
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD record or transcribe Quarterly Reviews and supply them to all necessary parties.

assessment_items.title: Schedule Around Reports
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD regularly schedule Quarterly Reviews to occur at least 3 business days after releasing an Ongoing Certification Report AND within 10 business days of such release.

assessment_items.title: Share Content Responsibly
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY responsibly supply content prepared for a Quarterly Review to the public or other parties if the provider determines doing so will NOT likely have an adverse effect on the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Share Recordings Responsibly
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY responsibly supply recordings or transcriptions of Quarterly Reviews to the public or other parties ONLY if the provider removes all agency information (comments, questions, names, etc.) AND determines doing so will NOT likely have an adverse effect on the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Availability Reporting
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications MUST maintain a web service, available to all necessary parties, that indicates current and historical availability of core services within the cloud service offering over at least the past 30 days, including availability incidents, in both human-readable and machine-readable formats; this service MUST be available even if the primary cloud service offering is unavailable.

assessment_items.title: Consistency Between Formats
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST use automation to ensure information remains consistent between human-readable and machine-readable formats when FedRAMP Certification Data is provided in both formats.

assessment_items.title: Always Include FedRAMP ID
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST always include the FedRAMP ID of the related cloud service offering in all FedRAMP Certification Data once assigned, including all reports, notifications, and other communication that results from FedRAMP rules.

assessment_items.title: FedRAMP Certification Reports
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST include FedRAMP Certification Reports with their FedRAMP Certification Data without inappropriate modifications, and make such reports available within 2 weeks of receiving the materials from FedRAMP.

assessment_items.title: Historical FedRAMP Certification Data
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply snapshots of FedRAMP Certification Data aligned to Ongoing Certification Reports to all necessary parties; these snapshots MUST be available for the duration of FedRAMP Certification.

assessment_items.title: Include Relevant Policies
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply all relevant policies and procedures in the FedRAMP Certification Data, including a human-readable and machine-readable reference that explains at least the following about each included policy and procedure:

assessment_items.title: Per-Service Certification Materials
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications MAY supply per-service FedRAMP Certification materials.

assessment_items.title: Public Information
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST publicly share up-to-date information about the cloud service offering in both human-readable and JSON formats, including at least the following information that is available and applicable:

assessment_items.title: Responsible Information Sharing
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST provide sufficient information in FedRAMP Certification Data to support agency authorization decisions but SHOULD NOT include sensitive information that would likely enable a threat actor to gain unauthorized access, cause harm, disrupt operations, or otherwise have a negative adverse impact on the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Responsible Public Package Sharing
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY responsibly share some or all of the information in a FedRAMP Certification Package publicly or with other parties if the provider determines doing so will NOT likely have an adverse effect on the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Public Service List
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST publicly share a detailed list of specific services and their security categories that are included in the cloud service offering using clear feature or service names that align with standard public marketing materials; this list MUST be complete enough for a potential customer to determine which services are and are not included in the FedRAMP Minimum Assessment Scope without requesting access to underlying FedRAMP Certification Data.

assessment_items.title: Use Trust Centers
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST use a FedRAMP-compatible trust center to store and share FedRAMP Certification Data with all necessary parties.

assessment_items.title: Agency Access Inventory
assessment_items.requirement_text: Trust centers MUST maintain an inventory and history of federal agency users or systems with access to FedRAMP Certification Data and MUST make this information available to FedRAMP upon request.

assessment_items.title: Access Logging
assessment_items.requirement_text: Trust centers MUST log access to FedRAMP Certification Data and store summaries of access for at least six months; such information, as it pertains to specific parties, SHOULD be made available upon request by those parties.

assessment_items.title: Human and Machine-Readable Certification Data
assessment_items.requirement_text: Trust centers SHOULD make FedRAMP Certification Data available to view and download in both human-readable and machine-readable formats.

assessment_items.title: Programmatic Access
assessment_items.requirement_text: Trust centers MUST provide documented programmatic access to all FedRAMP Certification Data, including programmatic access to human-readable materials.

assessment_items.title: Self-Service Access Management
assessment_items.requirement_text: Trust centers SHOULD include features that encourage all necessary parties to provision and manage access to FedRAMP Certification Data for their users and services directly.

assessment_items.title: Uninterrupted Sharing
assessment_items.requirement_text: Trust centers MUST share FedRAMP Certification Data with all necessary parties without interruption.

assessment_items.title: Agency Access Denial
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST notify FedRAMP within 5 business days of denying an agency access request for FedRAMP Certification Data.

assessment_items.title: Agency Access
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD supply access to the FedRAMP Certification Package with agencies upon request.

assessment_items.title: Configuration of Agency Tenants
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD configure agency tenants by default to use cryptographic services that use cryptographic modules or update streams of cryptographic modules with active validations under the NIST Cryptographic Module Validation Program when such modules are available.

assessment_items.title: Cryptographic Module Documentation
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST document the cryptographic modules used in each service (or groups of services that use the same modules) where cryptographic services are used to protect federal customer data, including whether these modules are validated under the NIST Cryptographic Module Validation Program or are update streams of such modules.

assessment_items.title: Using Validated Cryptographic Modules
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD use cryptographic modules or update streams of cryptographic modules with active validations under the NIST Cryptographic Module Validation Program when using cryptographic services to protect federal customer data.

assessment_items.title: Certification Package Overview Metadata
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST also include the following basic metadata in their Certification Package Overview:

assessment_items.title: Overall Summary of Assessment in Certification Package
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers seeking Class C Certification MUST also include the overall summary of their FedRAMP independent assessment, supplied by the assessor per IVV-IAS-OSA (Overall Summary of Assessment), in their Certification Package Overview.

assessment_items.title: Overview of the Cloud Service Offering
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply a Certification Package Overview within their FedRAMP Certification Package, in both human-readable and JSON formats, that includes at least all of the information required by the following rules:

assessment_items.title: Certification Package Maintenance for 20x
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with 20x Class C Certifications MUST persistently maintain their FedRAMP Certification Package to ensure it is up to date and complete at least once every 2 weeks.

assessment_items.title: Applying for FedRAMP Certification
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST complete the FedRAMP Certification Application Form in full to request an initial assessment by FedRAMP.

assessment_items.title: Fresh FedRAMP Certification Package
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply a fresh initial FedRAMP Certification Package that shows the current status of the cloud service offering as verified and validated by the provider within the previous 7 days.

assessment_items.title: Fresh Independent Assessment
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers seeking Class C Certification MUST supply a fresh initial FedRAMP independent assessment that was completed by a FedRAMP Recognized independent assessment service within the previous 3 months.

assessment_items.title: Marketplace Listing First
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST be listed in the FedRAMP Marketplace before applying for FedRAMP Certification, including:

assessment_items.title: No Third-Party Applicants
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST NOT use a third party to apply for a FedRAMP Certification on their behalf; this includes independent assessment services.

assessment_items.title: Updating Stale Assessments
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY freshen a stale initial independent verification and validation assessment by having a FedRAMP Recognized independent assessment service review any changes between the original assessment and the current status of the cloud service offering in place of a full re-assessment, UNLESS the stale assessment is more than 9 months old.

assessment_items.title: FedRAMP Certification Profile
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST identify a target FedRAMP Certification Profile and apply all relevant FedRAMP Practices to the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: FedRAMP JSON Schemas
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply machine-readable information in JSON documents that are valid against the corresponding JSON schema when a rule contains a FedRAMP JSON schema, UNLESS otherwise specified in the rule.

assessment_items.title: Maintain Responsibility and Accountability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST maintain responsibility and accountability for the accuracy and completeness of all information in the FedRAMP Certification Package, especially when they engage a third party (such as an independent assessor, advisory service, or external tools) to supply information on their behalf.

assessment_items.title: FedRAMP Certification Package
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers seeking a Certification MUST supply a complete FedRAMP Certification Package to FedRAMP for initial certification; the FedRAMP Certification Package MUST include at least the following information:

assessment_items.title: Pick One Program Certification Type
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST NOT seek both FedRAMP Rev5 Program Certification and FedRAMP 20x Program Certification for the same cloud service offering; pick one type.

assessment_items.title: Application within MAS
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD apply ALL Key Security Indicators to ALL aspects of their cloud service offering that are within the FedRAMP Minimum Assessment Scope.

assessment_items.title: Metrics Over Time for Key Security Indicators
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers seeking 20x Class C Certification MUST supply historical metrics including status from persistent validation over at least the past 6 months for all Key Security Indicators.

assessment_items.title: Automated Verification and Validation of Key Security Indicators
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers seeking 20x Class C Certification MUST implement automated methods to persistently verify and validate the accuracy and completeness of Key Security Indicators with at least 2 automated methods for each Key Security Indicator.

assessment_items.title: Automated Verification and Validation of FedRAMP Rules
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers seeking 20x Class C Certification SHOULD implement automated methods to persistently verify and validate the accuracy and completeness of the Security Decision Record for FedRAMP rules when applicable.

assessment_items.title: Automated Incident Reporting
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD use automation to minimize human intervention in the process of reporting FedRAMP Reportable Incidents to all affected parties.

assessment_items.title: Default PAIN Rating
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST treat FedRAMP Reportable Incidents as if they have a Potential Agency Impact N-rating (PAIN) of 5 UNLESS they promptly estimate the PAIN rating following the rule in IEC-CSO-EFI (Estimate Federal Impact).

assessment_items.title: Estimate Federal Impact
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD promptly estimate the likely adverse impact of an incident on agency customers to assign a Potential Agency Impact N-rating; this step is called Incident Rating.

assessment_items.title: Evaluate FedRAMP Reportability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST promptly evaluate incidents to determine if they affect confidentiality or integrity of federal customer data or are likely to affect confidentiality or integrity of federal customer data; such incidents are FedRAMP Reportable Incidents and must be reported following the FedRAMP Incident Evaluation and Communication rules.

assessment_items.title: Final Incident Report
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications MUST responsibly notify all affected parties by providing a Final Incident Report once the incident has been resolved and recovery is complete, including final updates to all previously reported information.

assessment_items.title: Initial Incident Report
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications MUST responsibly notify all affected parties after identifying FedRAMP Reportable Incidents by providing an Initial Incident Report with as much of the following information that is available at the time of reporting and/or the current relevant status for each item:

assessment_items.title: Ongoing Incident Reports
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications MUST responsibly notify all affected parties of ongoing activity as new information becomes available during incident response for FedRAMP Reportable Incidents, including updates (or lack of updates) to all previously reported information and as much of the the following additional information that is available and/or the current relevant status for each item:

assessment_items.title: Document Use of Representative Samples
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST document and explain the use of representative samples during verification and validation when using representative samples as allowed by IVV-CSO-USR (Use Representative Samples).

assessment_items.title: FedRAMP Independent Assessments
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications MUST persistently complete an independent verification and validation assessment of all applicable FedRAMP rules with a FedRAMP Recognized independent assessment service OR FedRAMP at least once per year; this is a FedRAMP independent assessment.

assessment_items.title: Inclusion in Certification Package
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply the results of FedRAMP independent assessments in their FedRAMP Certification Package without inappropriate modification.

assessment_items.title: Receiving Assessor Advice
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY ask for and accept advice from their assessor during assessment regarding techniques and procedures that will improve their security posture or the effectiveness, clarity, and accuracy of their verification, validation and reporting procedures, UNLESS doing so is likely to compromise the objectivity and integrity of the assessment.

assessment_items.title: Supply Evidence of Effectiveness
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply evidence to all necessary assessors of the effectiveness of the measures that have been implemented to meet FedRAMP Practices; this evidence is the result of validation.

assessment_items.title: Supply Evidence of Implementation
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply evidence to all necessary assessors of the implementation of the measures that have been documented to meet FedRAMP Practices; this evidence is the result of verification.

assessment_items.title: Supply Technical Explanations
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD supply all necessary assessors with technical explanations, demonstrations, and other relevant supporting information about the technical capabilities they employ to address FedRAMP rules; this SHOULD be supplied as necessary to ensure the assessor can effectively complete verification and validation.

assessment_items.title: Use Representative Samples
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY use representative samples as appropriate during verification and validation.

assessment_items.title: Annual Independent Assessments for 20x
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with 20x Class C Certifications MUST include all Key Security Indicators in a FedRAMP independent assessment at least once per year.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing All Training
assessment_items.requirement_text: The effectiveness of relevant cybersecurity education and training is persistently reviewed, including at least general training for all employees, role-specific training for employees in high risk roles, training for development and engineering staff on secure software delivery, and training for staff involved with incident response or disaster recovery.

assessment_items.title: Logging Changes
assessment_items.requirement_text: Modifications to the cloud service offering are logged and monitored.

assessment_items.title: Redeploying vs Modifying
assessment_items.requirement_text: Changes to machine-based information resources are executed through the redeployment of version controlled resources rather than direct modification wherever reasonable.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Change Procedures
assessment_items.requirement_text: The effectiveness of documented change management procedures is persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Validating Throughout Deployment
assessment_items.requirement_text: Persistent testing and validation of changes throughout deployment is automated.

assessment_items.title: Defining Functionality and Privileges
assessment_items.requirement_text: The functionality and privileges for infrastructure and services are strictly defined.

assessment_items.title: Enforcing Intended State
assessment_items.requirement_text: Automated services are used to persistently assess the security of all machine-based information resources and automatically enforce their intended operational state.

assessment_items.title: Implementing Best Practices
assessment_items.requirement_text: The use and configuration of third-party machine-based information resources is persistently compared against the original provider's best practices and guidance.

assessment_items.title: Minimizing Attack Surface
assessment_items.requirement_text: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they have a minimal attack surface and that lateral movement is minimized if compromised.

assessment_items.title: Optimizing for Availability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they are appropriately optimized for high availability and rapid recovery.

assessment_items.title: Restricting Network Traffic
assessment_items.requirement_text: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they are appropriately configured to limit inbound and outbound network traffic.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Protections
assessment_items.requirement_text: The effectiveness of protection against denial of service attacks and other unwanted activity for machine-based information resources is persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Using Logical Networking
assessment_items.requirement_text: Logical networking and related capabilities are used and persistently reviewed to enforce traffic flow controls.

assessment_items.title: Automating Account Management
assessment_items.requirement_text: The lifecycle and privileges of all accounts, roles, and groups are securely managed using automation.

assessment_items.title: Adopting Passwordless Methods
assessment_items.requirement_text: Secure passwordless methods are used for user authentication and authorization when feasible, otherwise strong passwords with phishing-resistant MFA is used.

assessment_items.title: Ensuring Least Privilege
assessment_items.requirement_text: Identity and access management measures are used and persistently reviewed to ensure each user or device can only access the resources they need.

assessment_items.title: Authorizing Just-in-Time
assessment_items.requirement_text: A least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time security authorization model is used and persistently reviewed for all user and non-user accounts and services.

assessment_items.title: Securing Non-User Authentication
assessment_items.requirement_text: Appropriately secure authentication methods are used and persistently reviewed for non-user accounts and services.

assessment_items.title: Responding to Suspicious Activity
assessment_items.requirement_text: Accounts with privileged access are disabled or otherwise secured in response to suspicious activity.

assessment_items.title: Generating After Action Reports
assessment_items.requirement_text: Incident after action reports are generated and lessons learned are persistently incorporated.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Incident Response Procedures
assessment_items.requirement_text: The effectiveness of documented incident response procedures is persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Past Incidents
assessment_items.requirement_text: Past incidents are persistently reviewed for patterns or vulnerabilities that were not previously apparent or identified.

assessment_items.title: Authorizing Log Access
assessment_items.requirement_text: A least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time access authorization model is used and persistently reviewed for access to log data based on organizationally defined data sensitivity.

assessment_items.title: Evaluating Configurations
assessment_items.requirement_text: The configuration of machine-based information resources, especially infrastructure as code, is persistently evaluated and tested.

assessment_items.title: Logging Event Types
assessment_items.requirement_text: A list of information resources and event types that will be logged, monitored, and audited is maintained and persistently reviewed to ensure these activities occur.

assessment_items.title: Operating SIEM Capability
assessment_items.requirement_text: A Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) or similar system(s) is used and persistently reviewed for centralized, tamper-resistant logging of events, activities, and changes.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Logs
assessment_items.requirement_text: Logs are persistently reviewed and audited.

assessment_items.title: Generating Inventories
assessment_items.requirement_text: Authoritative sources are used to automatically generate real-time inventories of all information resources when needed.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Executive Support
assessment_items.requirement_text: Executive support for achieving the provider's security goals is persistently reviewed and demonstrated.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Investments in Security
assessment_items.requirement_text: The effectiveness of the provider's investments in achieving security goals is persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Security in the SDLC
assessment_items.requirement_text: The effectiveness of building security and privacy considerations into the Software Development Lifecycle and aligning with CISA Secure By Design principles is persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Vulnerability Disclosures
assessment_items.requirement_text: The effectiveness of the provider's vulnerability disclosure program is persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Aligning Backups with Objectives
assessment_items.requirement_text: The alignment of machine-based information resource backups with defined recovery objectives is persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Aligning Recovery Plan
assessment_items.requirement_text: The alignment of recovery plans with defined recovery objectives is persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Reviewing Recovery Objectives
assessment_items.requirement_text: The desired Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) are defined and persistently reviewed for alignment with the provider's business needs and capabilities.

assessment_items.title: Testing Recovery Capabilities
assessment_items.requirement_text: The capability to recover from incidents and contingencies aligned with defined recovery objectives is persistently tested.

assessment_items.title: Mitigating Supply Chain Risk
assessment_items.requirement_text: Persistently identify, review, and mitigate potential supply chain risks.

assessment_items.title: Monitoring Supply Chain Risk
assessment_items.requirement_text: Third party software information resources are automatically monitored for upstream vulnerabilities using mechanisms that may include contractual notification requirements or active monitoring services.

assessment_items.title: Automating Configuration Management
assessment_items.requirement_text: The configuration of machine-based information resources is managed using automation and persistently reviewed for drift.

assessment_items.title: Automating Secret Management
assessment_items.requirement_text: Management, protection, and regular rotation of digital keys, certificates, and other secrets is automated and persistently reviewed.

assessment_items.title: Evaluating and Improving Security
assessment_items.requirement_text: Information resources are persistently evaluated for opportunities to improve security and those improvements are persistently made.

assessment_items.title: Preventing Residual Risk
assessment_items.requirement_text: Plans, procedures, and the state of information resources are persistently reviewed after making changes to limit and remove unwanted residual elements that would likely negatively affect the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of federal customer data.

assessment_items.title: Removing Unwanted Data
assessment_items.requirement_text: Unwanted federal customer data is removed promptly when requested by an agency in alignment with customer agreements, including from backups if appropriate; this typically applies when a customer spills information or when a customer seeks to remove information from a service due to a change in usage.

assessment_items.title: Securing Information
assessment_items.requirement_text: Information is encrypted or otherwise secured from unwanted access or modification.

assessment_items.title: Validating Communications
assessment_items.requirement_text: The authenticity and integrity of communications between machine-based information resources is persistently validated using automation.

assessment_items.title: Validating Resource Integrity
assessment_items.requirement_text: Use cryptographic methods to validate the integrity of machine-based information resources.

assessment_items.title: Information Flows and Security Categories
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST clearly identify, document, and explain information flows and security categories for ALL information resources or sets of information resources in the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Identify Information Resources
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST identify a set of information resources to assess for FedRAMP Certification that includes all information resources that are likely to handle federal customer data or likely to impact the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of federal customer data handled by the cloud service offering; this set of information resources is the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Metadata Inclusion
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST include metadata (including metadata about federal customer data) in the Minimum Assessment Scope ONLY IF MAS-CSO-IIR (Identify Information Resources) APPLIES.

assessment_items.title: Supplemental Information
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY include additional materials about other information resources that are not part of the cloud service offering in a FedRAMP Certification Package supplement; these resources will not be FedRAMP Certified and MUST be clearly marked and separated from the cloud service offering.

assessment_items.title: Third-Party Information Resources
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST address the potential impact to federal customer data from third-party information resources used by the cloud service offering, ONLY IF MAS-CSO-IIR (Identify Information Resources) APPLIES, by documenting the following information about each applicable third-party information resource:

assessment_items.title: Marketplace Listing Requirements
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST address at least these FedRAMP rules to apply for a new FedRAMP Marketplace listing OR to request updates to an existing listing:

assessment_items.title: Provider Marketplace Listing Requests
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST notify FedRAMP using the FedRAMP Marketplace Providing Listing Request Form to request a listing in the FedRAMP Marketplace.

assessment_items.title: Use Instructions
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST include instructions in the FedRAMP Certification Package that explain how to obtain and use the Secure Configuration Guide.

assessment_items.title: Public Secure Configuration Guidance
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD make the Secure Configuration Guide available publicly.

assessment_items.title: Recommended Secure Configuration
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST create, maintain, and make available recommendations for securely configuring their cloud services (the Secure Configuration Guide) that includes at least the following information:

assessment_items.title: Secure Defaults
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD set all settings to their recommended secure defaults for top-level administrative accounts and privileged accounts when initially provisioned.

assessment_items.title: API Capability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD offer the capability to view and adjust security settings via an API or similar capability.

assessment_items.title: Comparison Capability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD offer the capability to compare all current settings for top-level administrative accounts and privileged accounts to the recommended secure defaults.

assessment_items.title: Export Capability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD offer the capability to export all security settings in a machine-readable format.

assessment_items.title: Machine-Readable Guidance
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD also provide the Secure Configuration Guide in a machine-readable format that can be used by customers or third-party tools to compare against current settings.

assessment_items.title: Versioning and Release History
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD provide versioning and a release history for recommended secure default settings for top-level administrative accounts and privileged accounts as they are adjusted over time.

assessment_items.title: Notification Requirements
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST notify all necessary parties within 10 business days after finishing adaptive changes, also including the following information:

assessment_items.title: Additional Relevant Information
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY include additional relevant information in Significant Change Notifications.

assessment_items.title: Emergency Changes
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY execute significant changes (including transformative changes) during an emergency or incident without following the Significant Change Notification rules in advance. In such emergencies, providers MUST follow all relevant procedures, notify all necessary parties, retroactively provide all Significant Change Notification materials, and complete appropriate assessment after the incident.

assessment_items.title: Evaluate Changes
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST evaluate all potential significant changes to determine the type of significant change and follow the appropriate Significant Change Notification rules.

assessment_items.title: Historical Notifications
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST keep 12 months of historical Significant Change Notifications available with their FedRAMP Certification Data.

assessment_items.title: Human and Machine-Readable Notifications
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST make ALL Significant Change Notifications and related audit records available in human-readable and JSON formats.

assessment_items.title: Required Information
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST include at least the following information in Significant Change Notifications:

assessment_items.title: Maintain Audit Records
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST maintain auditable records of the significant change evaluation activities required by SCN-CSO-EVA (Evaluate Changes) and make them available to FedRAMP as requested.

assessment_items.title: Notification Mechanisms
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY notify necessary parties in a variety of ways as long as the mechanism for notification is clearly documented in the FedRAMP Certification Package and easily accessible.

assessment_items.title: No Notification Requirements
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD NOT make formal Significant Change Notifications for routine recurring changes; this type of change is exempted from notification requirements.

assessment_items.title: Notification After Finishing
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST notify all necessary parties within 5 business days after finishing transformative changes, including updates to all previously sent information.

assessment_items.title: Notification After Verification
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST notify all necessary parties within 5 business days after completing the verification, assessment, and/or validation of transformative changes, also including the following information:

assessment_items.title: Notification of Final Plans
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST notify all necessary parties of final plans for transformative changes at least 10 business days before starting transformative changes, including updates to all previously sent information.

assessment_items.title: Notification of Initial Plans
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST notify all necessary parties of initial plans for transformative changes at least 30 business days before starting transformative changes, including a summary of any likely security impacts or changes in risk.

assessment_items.title: Third-Party Review
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD engage a third-party assessor to review the scope and impact of the planned change before starting transformative changes if human validation is necessary; such reviews SHOULD be limited to security decisions that require human validation.

assessment_items.title: Update Documentation
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST publish updated service documentation and other materials to reflect transformative changes within 30 business days after finishing transformative changes.

assessment_items.title: FedRAMP Rules
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST supply a Security Decision Record, in both human-readable and JSON formats, that includes at least all of the following information for each applicable FedRAMP rule:

assessment_items.title: Security Decision Record Metadata
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST also include the following basic metadata in their Security Decision Record:

assessment_items.title: Key Security Indicator Metrics
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with 20x Class C Certifications MUST also include historical metrics in their Security Decision Record, supplying at least the following information for each applicable Key Security Indicator:

assessment_items.title: Key Security Indicators
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST also include short and simple high-level summaries of at least the following for each applicable Key Security Indicator:

assessment_items.title: Automate Detection
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD use automated services to improve and streamline vulnerability detection and response.

assessment_items.title: Avoid KEVs
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD NOT deploy or otherwise activate new machine-based information resources with Known Exploited Vulnerabilities.

assessment_items.title: Detect After Changes
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD automatically perform vulnerability detection on representative samples of new or significantly changed information resources.

assessment_items.title: Vulnerability Detection
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST systematically, persistently, and promptly discover and identify vulnerabilities within their cloud service offering using appropriate techniques such as assessment, scanning, threat intelligence, vulnerability disclosure mechanisms, bug bounties, penetration testing, incident response, automated control testing, supply chain monitoring, and other relevant capabilities; this process is called vulnerability detection. Vulnerability detection includes persistently verifying and validating that information resources and processes are operating as intended and documented for FedRAMP Practices.

assessment_items.title: Design For Resilience
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD make design and architecture decisions for their cloud service offering that mitigate the risk of vulnerabilities by default AND decrease the risk and complexity of vulnerability detection and response.

assessment_items.title: Failures Are Vulnerabilities
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST treat problems or failures with their vulnerability detection and response processes as vulnerabilities.

assessment_items.title: Maintain Security
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD NOT weaken the security of information resources to facilitate vulnerability scanning, detection, or assessment activities.

assessment_items.title: Vulnerability Response
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST systematically, persistently, and promptly track, evaluate, monitor, mitigate, remediate, assess exploitation of, report, and otherwise manage all detected vulnerabilities within their cloud service offering; this process is called vulnerability response.

assessment_items.title: Sampling
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY sample effectively identical information resources, especially machine-based information resources, when performing vulnerability detection UNLESS doing so would decrease the efficiency or effectiveness of vulnerability detection.

assessment_items.title: Remediate KEVs
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD remediate Known Exploited Vulnerabilities according to the due dates in the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog (even if the vulnerability has been fully mitigated) as required by CISA Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04 or any successor guidance from CISA.

assessment_items.title: Persistent Machine Verification and Validation for 20x
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers of FedRAMP 20x Class C offerings MUST verify and validate the status of machine-based information resources at least once every 3 days.

assessment_items.title: Non-Machine Verification and Validation
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST verify and validate the status of non-machine-based information resources at least once every 3 months.

assessment_items.title: Persistently Complete Detection
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD persistently perform vulnerability detection on all information resources that are NOT likely to drift, at least once every month.

assessment_items.title: Persistent Drift Detection
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD persistently perform vulnerability detection on all information resources that are likely to drift, at least once every 14 days.

assessment_items.title: Persistent Sample Detection
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD persistently perform vulnerability detection on representative samples of similar machine-based information resources, at least once every 3 days.

assessment_items.title: Mitigation and Remediation Expectations
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD partially mitigate vulnerabilities, fully mitigate vulnerabilities, or remediate vulnerabilities to a lower Potential Agency Impact N-rating within the timeframes from evaluation shown below, factoring for the current Potential Agency Impact N-rating as defined in VER-EVA-EPA (Estimate Potential Agency Impact), internet reachability, and likely exploitability:

assessment_items.title: Remaining Vulnerabilities
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD mitigate or remediate remaining vulnerabilities during routine operations as determined necessary by the provider.

assessment_items.title: Assume It's Automatable
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST assume the exploitation of vulnerabilities can be automated UNLESS they have evidence proving otherwise.

assessment_items.title: Evaluation Factors
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD consider at least the following factors when considering the context of the cloud service offering to evaluate detected vulnerabilities:

assessment_items.title: Evaluate False Positives
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD evaluate detected vulnerabilities, considering the context of the cloud service offering, to determine if they are false positive vulnerabilities.

assessment_items.title: Evaluate Internet-Reachability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST evaluate detected vulnerabilities, considering the context of the cloud service offering, to determine if they are internet-reachable vulnerabilities.

assessment_items.title: Evaluate Exploitability
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST evaluate detected vulnerabilities, considering the context of the cloud service offering, to determine if they are likely exploitable vulnerabilities.

assessment_items.title: Estimate Potential Agency Impact
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST evaluate detected vulnerabilities, considering the context of the cloud service offering, to estimate the potential agency impact of exploitation on government customers AND assign one of the following Potential Agency Impact N-ratings (PAIN):

assessment_items.title: Group Vulnerabilities
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD evaluate detected vulnerabilities, considering the context of the cloud service offering, to identify logical groupings of affected information resources that may improve the efficiency and effectiveness of vulnerability response by consolidating further activity; FedRAMP Vulnerability Detection and Response rules are then applied to these consolidated groupings of vulnerabilities instead of each individual detected instance.

assessment_items.title: Accepted Vulnerability Info
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST include the following information on accepted vulnerabilities when reporting on vulnerability detection and response activity:

assessment_items.title: High-Level Overviews
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers SHOULD include high-level overviews of ALL vulnerability detection and response activities conducted during this period for the cloud service offering; this includes vulnerability disclosure programs, bug bounty programs, penetration testing, assessments, etc.

assessment_items.title: Responsible Disclosure
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST NOT irresponsibly disclose specific sensitive information about vulnerabilities that would likely lead to exploitation, but MUST disclose sufficient information for informed risk-based decision-making to all necessary parties.

assessment_items.title: Persistent Reporting
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST report vulnerability detection and response activity (including persistent verification and validation) to all necessary parties persistently, summarizing ALL activity since the previous report; these reports are FedRAMP Certification Data and are subject to FedRAMP Certification Data Sharing rules.

assessment_items.title: Responsible Public Disclosure
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MAY responsibly disclose vulnerabilities publicly or with other parties if the provider determines doing so will NOT likely lead to exploitation.

assessment_items.title: Vulnerability Details
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST include the following information (if applicable) on detected vulnerabilities when reporting on vulnerability detection and response activity, UNLESS it is an accepted vulnerability:

assessment_items.title: Evaluate Vulnerabilities Quickly
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD evaluate ALL vulnerabilities as required by VER-EVA (Evaluation) within 5 days of detection.

assessment_items.title: Internet-Reachable Incidents
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD treat internet-reachable likely exploitable vulnerabilities where Potential Agency Impact N-rating > 3 as a FedRAMP Reportable Incident until they are partially mitigated vulnerabilities at N3 or below.

assessment_items.title: Mark Accepted Vulnerabilities
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST categorize any vulnerability that is not or will not be fully mitigated or remediated within 192 days of evaluation as an accepted vulnerability.

assessment_items.title: Monthly Activity Report
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers MUST report vulnerability detection and response activity to all necessary parties in a consistent format that is human readable at least monthly.

assessment_items.title: Historical Activity
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD make all recent historical vulnerability detection and response activity available in JSON format for automated retrieval by all necessary parties (e.g. using an API service or similar); this information SHOULD be updated persistently, at least once every 14 days.

assessment_items.title: Non-Internet-Reachable Incidents
assessment_items.requirement_text: Providers with Class C Certifications MAY treat likely exploitable vulnerabilities that are NOT internet-reachable where Potential Agency Impact N-rating = 5 as a FedRAMP Reportable Incident until they are partially mitigated vulnerabilities at N4 or below.

artifact_requirements.display_name: Key Security Indicator Methods and Metric History
